name: =?utf-8?B?SmFoYW5h4oCZcyBpUGhvbmU=?=
subject: I know that I need to start working now if we want to win
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes
body:
Friends, the 2024 election is creeping up on us every day, and as one of just a few “Frontline” members of Congress, I know that I need to start working now if we want to win this re-election campaign.

As a frontliner, that means that experts agree that our race will not only be one of the most competitive in the country – but the most one of the expensive, as well.

We won last year thanks to all the hard work my grassroots donors and campaign volunteers put in, but we need to prepare for an even harder fight next year. This community deserves a leader who will listen and fights for our future through education, housing, infrastructure, economic relief, and so much more.

But we need to make sure that message reaches every single person in my district, and if the 2024 election is anything like last year, we need to prepare for millions of dollars pouring into this district.

So please, team, we’re just about halfway through the second quarter of the year and my team says we need to fundraise even harder if we want to reach our goals. It is so important that we continue building on this momentum!

If you can, please chip in $25 or more towards my re-election campaign so we can push back against the outside money that will flood into this highly competitive “Frontline” district.

You can give here: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/JHCT-05-Email-May2023-FR-May?refcode=E21523-20230522-SecondQuarter-SL-DeadlineOther-PlainText-NA-NA&amount=25

Thank you,

Jahana
Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes

As a frontliner, that means that experts agree that our race will not only be one of the most competitive in the country – but the most one of the expensive, as well. We won last year thanks to all the hard work my grassroots donors and campaign volunteers put in, but we need to prepare for an even harder fight next year. This community deserves a leader who will listen and fights for our future through education, housing, infrastructure, economic relief, and so much more. But we need to make sure that message reaches every single person in my district, and if the 2024 election is anything like last year, we need to prepare for millions of dollars pouring into this district. So please, team, we’re just about halfway through the second quarter of the year and my team says we need to fundraise even harder if we want to reach our goals. It is so important that we continue building on this momentum! If you can, please chip in $25 or more towards my re-election campaign so we can push back against the outside money that will flood into this highly competitive “Frontline” district. You can give here: Thank you, Jahana Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: . Friends of Jahana Hayes P.O. Box 1487 Waterbury, CT 06721 United States

name: Peter Welch
subject: What’s on your mind?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WELCH FOR VERMONT
body:
I want to hear from you

# [](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/144?t=2&akid=1264%2E61531%2E-Oistz)
Hi There,
There’s been so much in the news lately — potential default, ongoing ethics issues with the Supreme Court, abortion, and book bans around the country.
I’ve been working hard in the Senate to lower costs for working families, combat climate change, take on Big Pharma, and more. But I want to stay updated on what’s important to you.
[**Will you take my survey so I can hear what’s on your mind? I want to hear from my closest supporters.**](https://act.welchforvermont.com/survey/em-whats-on-your-mind-survey/?t=3&akid=1264%2E61531%2E-Oistz)
[Take our survey ](https://act.welchforvermont.com/survey/em-whats-on-your-mind-survey/?t=7&t=8&akid=1264%2E61531%2E-Oistz)
---
Throughout the campaign last year, I traveled around Vermont and met so many of you to hear about the issues that you cared about. I heard from parents who were struggling to pay for child care, patients who couldn’t afford their medication, and young people worried about the future of our planet.
It all helped me prioritize what to work on in the Senate. But the work doesn’t stop there. I want to continue hearing from you about the issues that matter most.
[**So, will you take my survey so I know what your top issues are right now?**](https://act.welchforvermont.com/survey/em-whats-on-your-mind-survey/?t=4&akid=1264%2E61531%2E-Oistz)
Looking forward to hearing from you,
Peter

---

---

Peter,

Earlier this month, Florida Republicans introduced one of the strictest anti-immigration bills in history.

The bill would require hospitals to ask patients about their citizenship status, ban non-citizens from acquiring driver’s licenses, and prohibit undocumented immigrants from being admitted to practice law in the state.

Amidst an already hostile environment towards immigrants, this egregious bill would further push hundreds of thousands of people into the shadows. [_**We simply cannot stand by and allow Republicans to push their extremist, xenophobic agenda.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63666432/407567608/-1382951893?refcode=BLU-SAN-20230522-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODI2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMmY0Zjc0Mi1kYmY4LWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EUMhURPNkSWuuwLqH-vxxvkq6QLLwBQOlce1K1lPBvU=)

Jason will always put Coloradans first, which is why it’s so important we hear from you.

But with Republican obstruction at an all-time high, we can’t take anything for granted.

clean_body: Jason Crow For Congress [] We want to make sure you had a chance to leave your feedback in our new Democratic Priorities Survey. Jason would love to hear from you. Will you take a minute and complete our Democratic Priorities survey now? BEGIN THE SURVEY >> [] Team, It’s not enough to just talk the talk. Members of Congress need to listen and actually solve the problems their communities are facing. Jason promised to turn the page when he was elected, and he’s kept his word. He travels back to the district, holds town halls, and engages with the community every chance he gets. Jason will always put Coloradans first, which is why it’s so important we hear from you. Will you complete our Democratic Priorities Survey? Your responses today will help drive our campaign strategy: [] BEGIN THE SURVEY >> [] Jason is committed to addressing and solving the problems that everyday Coloradans face. He’s brought leadership and results from day one. - Supporting Colorado’s small businesses - Defending our democracy at home and abroad — calling out dangerous actors like Donald Trump and his far-right extremists But with Republican obstruction at an all-time high, we can’t take anything for granted. Share your feedback in our Democratic Priorities Survey today. [] Thanks, Team Crow Now more than ever, we need strong leaders like Jason working for us in Congress. Jason is fighting for you, your family, and the American people in Congress — and he's not taking a dime of corporate PAC money. Will you pitch in now to help him continue working for us? | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63667043/407571505/765663662?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjVmMDMxMC1kZGY4LWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YAoaTIP5oH5CaaYFtmQexyZRwIXq9zSn7NTOXKUYLQ0=&emci=1eaa1cdd-c5f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=cb5f0310-ddf8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=561582) | Team, Soon, schools across the U.S. will close for the summer. For many, this means summer vacations, camp, or pool time, but for parents who can’t afford to pay for childcare or have to work multiple minimum-wage jobs to make ends meet, summer brings with it a new set of challenges. As we gear up for summer, it’s critical that _every parent_ has the support they need. [_**Will you sign our petition to raise wages and expand parental leave policies for working people in America, friend?**_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63667044/407571506/-639144637?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjVmMDMxMC1kZGY4LWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YAoaTIP5oH5CaaYFtmQexyZRwIXq9zSn7NTOXKUYLQ0=&emci=1eaa1cdd-c5f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=cb5f0310-ddf8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=561582) [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63667045/407571507/-639144637?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjVmMDMxMC1kZGY4LWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YAoaTIP5oH5CaaYFtmQexyZRwIXq9zSn7NTOXKUYLQ0=&emci=1eaa1cdd-c5f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=cb5f0310-ddf8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=561582) The school year provides many working parents with a place to care for their children, accessible lunches, and after-school programs. Working parents need support and stability year-round, not just during the school year — and raising wages and enacting other policies to support parents is one way we can do that. [_**Will you sign our petition to raise wages and expand parental leave policies for working people in America, friend?**_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63667046/407571508/-639144637?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjVmMDMxMC1kZGY4LWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YAoaTIP5oH5CaaYFtmQexyZRwIXq9zSn7NTOXKUYLQ0=&emci=1eaa1cdd-c5f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=cb5f0310-ddf8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=561582) [SIGN THE PETITION](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63667047/407571509/-639144637?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjVmMDMxMC1kZGY4LWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YAoaTIP5oH5CaaYFtmQexyZRwIXq9zSn7NTOXKUYLQ0=&emci=1eaa1cdd-c5f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=cb5f0310-ddf8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=561582) — FP Team

clean_body: | | Team, Soon, schools across the U.S. will close for the summer. For many, this means summer vacations, camp, or pool time, but for parents who can’t afford to pay for childcare or have to work multiple minimum-wage jobs to make ends meet, summer brings with it a new set of challenges. As we gear up for summer, it’s critical that every parent has the support they need. The school year provides many working parents with a place to care for their children, accessible lunches, and after-school programs. Working parents need support and stability year-round, not just during the school year — and raising wages and enacting other policies to support parents is one way we can do that. — FP Team Thanks to grassroots supporters like you, our team at the Fairness Project has won 31 people-powered ballot measure campaigns since 2016, changing over 18 million lives by expanding health care, raising wages, guaranteeing paid leave, and ending predatory lending directly at the ballot box when politicians refuse to act. Your support allows us to make this progress possible.** = = Contributions or gifts to the Fairness Project are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. Contributions to the Fairness Project support its many efforts nationwide and any focus is at the Fairness Project’s sole discretion. Contributions will not necessarily be used to support activity in any one state. You can request to get only the most important emails from our team , or you can . body:
We’re both in Congress thanks to the same team that showed up and turned out to make history and send us to Washington.
| Hey Derek,

Congressman Chris Deluzio and I have a lot in common: Yinzers, Iron City Beer enjoyers, and fighters for working people in PA. 💪

That’s why I’m proud to be representing Pennsylvania in Washington alongside Chris, fighting for abortion rights, good jobs, and everything else that matters to folks in every corner of PA.

We’re both in Congress thanks to the same team that showed up and turned out to make history and send us to Washington. And that means the only way we’ll be able to keep up this fight is with your help.

name: Callie Barr
subject: Veterans as political pawns? Not on my watch.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Callie Barr for Congress
body:
Rep. Jack Bergman voted to slash veterans’ services by 22%. As a military spouse who's fought against red tape and bureaucracy, I’m outraged. | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63667407/407572648/1201310784?refcode=fl_em_fr_20230522vacutsRS_cb_full_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDUwMDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDY5ZmJkYmMtZGVmOC1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=FE0wviviZuhHhl-UFKmMf1Kk8rTa7ZEvpXffzhRinVo=&emci=89306d6f-5bf6-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=069fbdbc-def8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=9697413)
---
Hey Peter, Rep. Jack Bergman voted to force an extremist agenda that will **slash veterans’ services by 22%.** **As a military spouse who's fought against red tape and bureaucracy that kept my husband Matt and thousands like him from getting the healthcare they needed, I’m outraged that our current congressman would even consider a proposal that would make it even harder for veterans to get lifesaving care.** My husband Matt's service in Iraq resulted in a traumatic brain injury and PTSD – a diagnosis that took years for us to get. Getting Matt the right treatment and affordable care he needed was hard, and watching him suffer was even harder. That's why I went to work for Blue Star Families, to give a voice to military families like mine. And when I became a lawyer, I used my position at one of Michigan's top law firms to help veterans get free legal services. **This is personal.** **[That's why I'm running, and it's why I'm asking you to pitch in your first donation of $5 or whatever you can to help send me to Congress.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63667408/407572649/-621152178?refcode=fl_em_fr_20230522vacutsRS_cb_full_xx_l1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDUwMDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDY5ZmJkYmMtZGVmOC1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=FE0wviviZuhHhl-UFKmMf1Kk8rTa7ZEvpXffzhRinVo=)** | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

When we launched our campaign for Congress, I committed myself to standing up for people in this district, working to solve problems – not create them. This is exactly why we need to change who is representing us in Congress.
Our veterans – all of us – deserve better. And I'm determined to make it happen with your help.
Thank you,
Callie Barr
Candidate
Michigan’s 1st Congressional District

This is personal. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = | = ---|---|--- = | = | = When we launched our campaign for Congress, I committed myself to standing up for people in this district, working to solve problems – not create them. This is exactly why we need to change who is representing us in Congress. Our veterans – all of us – deserve better. And I'm determined to make it happen with your help. Thank you, Callie Barr Candidate Michigan’s 1st Congressional District | Callie Barr is a 5th-generation Michigander, advocate for veterans, and lawyer. She’s running to take on extremism and do-nothing politicians in D.C. that are leaving rural America behind. Can you help send Callie to Congress by donating today? | = | Paid for by Callie Barr for Congress Callie Barr for Congress PO Box 6921 Traverse City, MI 49696 United States Callie Barr is an advocate for veterans and spouse to a former U.S. Marine. subject: I feel like I’m taking crazy pills
disclaimer_text: Paid for by 1776 Project PAC
body:
I’m tired of watching the GOP use the same tired, losing strategies over and over again.

https://secure.winred.com/1776-project-pac/crazy-pills-av/?utm_source=prospecting&utm_medium=ha-email&utm_campaign=20230517_e000532-ss_crazy-pills-av_av&utm_content=av&recurring=true&amount=

Friend, I’m exhausted.

For too long the GOP has used the same tired, losing strategies that have led us to defeat after crushing defeat.

But, Friend, what has the left done that we haven’t? (https://secure.winred.com/1776-project-pac/crazy-pills-av/?utm_source=prospecting&utm_medium=ha-email&utm_campaign=20230517_e000532-ss_crazy-pills-av_av&utm_content=av&recurring=true&amount=)

The radical left has made an historic incursion into our schools, and they’ve done so with NO resistance from conservatives.

And for DECADES now leftists have had TOTAL CONTROL over what our children learn in schools.

Yet conservatives expect to win when the next generation is being TRAINED to hate conservatism, and basic American Values!

I’ll put this simply: Conservatism and Love for American Values will face a mass extinction event if we don’t stand up NOW!

My name is Ryan Girdusky, and I founded the 1776 Project PAC to fight back against the radical left and take back our schools one by one.

And in just over a year, we’ve notched more than 130 victories.

And what’s even more astonishing is that we’ve done it for PENNIES on the dollar compared to what other organizations have spent on congressional races that NEVER achieve anything of substance.

We’ve been so successful that people like Tucker Carlson, Ben Shapiro, and Laura Ingraham have even publicly praised our organization and encouraged their viewers to support our team.

Our team has already achieved an amazing level of success... Just imagine what we could do with you on our team!

Would you consider joining the 1776 Project PAC with a donation of $17.76 or more today? (https://secure.winred.com/1776-project-pac/crazy-pills-av/?utm_source=prospecting&utm_medium=ha-email&utm_campaign=20230517_e000532-ss_crazy-pills-av_av&utm_content=av&recurring=true&amount=)

Thousands of patriots like you have already decided to step up and make a difference- and their support has enabled us to impact the lives of MILLIONS of students, and change the future of America.

Please, {{#if firstname}}{{firstname}}{{else}}Friend{{/if}}, will you join us in taking back our schools once and for all?

Sincerely,

Ryan Girdusky, Founder

1776 Project PAC

https://secure.winred.com/1776-project-pac/crazy-pills-av/?utm_source=prospecting&utm_medium=ha-email&utm_campaign=20230517_e000532-ss_crazy-pills-av_av&utm_content=av&recurring=true&amount=

Please consider making a critical donation to the 1776 Project PAC today (https://secure.winred.com/1776-project-pac/crazy-pills-av/?utm_source=prospecting&utm_medium=ha-email&utm_campaign=20230517_e000532-ss_crazy-pills-av_av&utm_content=av&recurring=true&amount=)

184 7th Ave #106, New York, NY 10011

Friend, I believe our country is hungry for positive, conservative leadership.

It is time for us to restore hope, create opportunities, and protect America. **It is time for us to be proud to be Americans and proud of our president.**

**_[That's why I announced I am running for President of the United States.](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=e555ac081bc84505a7e6265e12906f35&_e=aM71SurLLutGv8gg9tPG-aGRbhoeJRntIpRtlAWeGr_eQt1WoJnQxjlXp1iAk0NUKvbVYq3X5-gctS9NLVbkWWLQ6Me2PZLuIGKV_iGbxYj6pLQ3G2H-enRzqA3C1kxLqIbNOjm_AEzryZra5wMx41B0SVyyfCuHkGocCZkHjAVCqqjzVxGHOVRxaYUnhThqcQHR5TSvcUVxfmqeHRgjdS4Dm2C21DPPBxAiCFV6sufba-YzrzYOXFR_HMVdIBGDxGW0k0KQYBKqv90aYjym9YKWuStlShBL6eVLOtStRM8Fd-stZI84ZWI572O9bdODNUHPAj0QkUznjqbeu6kQXuJE0sBr7c1C-mieJklSaZO8xynJyvdp8K3OvMZRo7vVFnhgrTWwPguZ5uTrq_Lz0b_L0HEIx6K7sXJFRMdR1vO_eSCEGnr_sTytu_7D8ERWfjaZWSf4FXMVr9a4e4eq0A%3D%3D "That’s why I announced I am running for President of the United States.")_**

---
| | [SUPPORT MY CAMPAIGN HERE](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=e555ac081bc84505a7e6265e12906f35&_e=aM71SurLLutGv8gg9tPG-aGRbhoeJRntIpRtlAWeGr_eQt1WoJnQxjlXp1iAk0NUKvbVYq3X5-gctS9NLVbkWWLQ6Me2PZLuIGKV_iGbxYj6pLQ3G2H-enRzqA3C1kxLqIbNOjm_AEzryZra5wMx41B0SVyyfCuHkGocCZkHjAVCqqjzVxGHOVRxaYUnhThqcQHR5TSvcUVxfmqeHRgjdS4Dm2C21DPPBxAiCFV6sufba-YzrzYOXFR_HMVdIBGDxGW0k0KQYBKqv90aYjym9YKWuStlShBL6eVLOtStRM8Fd-stZI84ZWI572O9bdODpGsL8Onxplq16gH0FCqdI-ozaoOTc5qgCahGPC6z7RjMWlhMp0sne-S3mNeFpMXDyanfCkrFmQyoiZs9GU2IeZ7V8DvE9Kd7q_DonY2TVA-994URyoQipZOT-k2kKctKksuTjPBZtC-IjSFPRkREGA%3D%3D "SUPPORT MY CAMPAIGN HERE")

The Democrats have chosen a culture of grievance over greatness. Here's the thing though - I know the power of the American Dream _because I have lived it._ My family went from cotton to congress in a **single lifetime.**

**This isn 't going to be easy.** I'll need your help to win.

| | Winning this competitive race and electing Angela to the U.S. Senate is going to take a village, Peter, so we want to hear from you: That’s why we put together a short, 5-question survey so that we can build the best campaign possible. **[Will you take our quick survey and let us know what issues matter most to you?](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/sign/EM_BS_AAL_230522_EN_IssuesSurvey_A0_na_U1_X1_M1?t=7&akid=75%2E52803%2EFLqS2H)** | | | [Take the Survey >>](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/sign/EM_BS_AAL_230522_EN_IssuesSurvey_A0_na_U1_X1_M1/?t=8&akid=75%2E52803%2EFLqS2H)

**[Can you spare 1 minute to make your voice heard and share your input with Angela and her team?](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/sign/EM_BS_AAL_230522_EN_IssuesSurvey_A0_na_U1_X1_M1/?t=9&akid=75%2E52803%2EFLqS2H)**

name: =?utf-8?B?UmFxdWVsIFRlcsOhbg==?=
subject: The climate crisis demands action NOW
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Raquel Terán for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/raquelteran2024?refcode=X_052222_ClimateCrisis&amount=10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/raquelteran2024?refcode=X_052222_ClimateCrisis&amount=10]
Hi friend,
We are about to reach a milestone none of us should be celebrating. According to the UN, global warming is set to increase temperatures by 2.7 degrees Fahrenheit within the next five years, a threshold that scientists warn is the upper limit before our planet hits a key climate tipping point.
The climate crisis is not some distant, vague threat in the future. We are already dealing with its consequences now. Arizonans, in many ways, are at the frontlines of it, dealing with record-breaking heat, dryer weather, and water shortages.
Imagine the future our children and grandchildren could face if we delay action any further. As a mom, I’m not going to let that happen. Our leaders must speak up and take bold action on the climate crisis. In Congress, you can bet that tackling climate change will be one of my top priorities.
But we can’t get to Congress if we don’t have the resources to win our election. So, friend, can you join our team and chip in a few bucks today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/raquelteran2024?refcode=X_052222_ClimateCrisis&amount=10]
There is no sugar-coating it. Our state is already facing the consequences of the climate crisis, but I know it also presents us with a great opportunity. By taking advantage of our state’s solar energy potential, investing in new technologies like water recycling, and improving public transportation, we can show our resilience as a state and as a people.
For my son and all our kids, we can and must take action. Join our grassroots campaign today. Chip in a few dollars >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/raquelteran2024?refcode=X_052222_ClimateCrisis&amount=10]
A ganar,
Raquel Terán
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/raquelteran2024?refcode=X_052222_ClimateCrisis&amount=10]
Raquel Terán for Congress
PO Box 1041
Glendale, AZ 85311
United States
Paid for by Raquel Terán for Congress

name: Lexie, Tammy Baldwin for Senate
subject: Fundraising update:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1001&akid=318%2E35263%2EifCinp ]TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE

Hey Peter — It’s Lexie, Tammy’s Finance Director.

Every morning, our finance team meets for about 15 minutes to check in on
the status toward our goals and talk through our priorities for the week.
And sometimes, we close the meeting with a fun “question of the day” —
today, we debated hot or iced coffee ☕

These meetings are typically my favorite part of the day. I love getting
to share good news with the team about how supporters like you are helping
us hit our fundraising targets and powering our work to reelect Tammy next
November.

But unfortunately, I had some not-so-great news to share this morning.
We’re tracking pretty far behind on our target this month... With just over
a week left before our end-of-month deadline, we’re only 40% of the way to
our online donation goal.

Now, I'll be honest, coming up short on a fundraising goal now and then
isn’t the end of the world — but it does have very real consequences for
our campaign. Every dollar we raise helps us fund our digital ads, print
campaign materials, and hire more staff so we can get Tammy’s message out
to voters in every stitch of the state. And when we’re coming up short,
it’s my job to start making cuts.

But I’m taking a risk this month. I told our team that we’re going to hold
off on lowering our goals or making budget cuts just yet because I know
we’ve got a really strong group of supporters behind us, and with your
help, I truly believe that we can get back on track.

[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2155?c0=tbe-em-may23.WiYufF&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230522_PM1_V1_S1&t=1004&akid=318%2E35263%2EifCinp ]So Peter, will you chip in $5, $10, or anything you can to
help us close the gap in our fundraising this month? I’d really appreciate
it.

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2150?c0=tbe-em-may23.gvrN3l&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230522_PM1_V1_S1&t=1005&akid=318%2E35263%2EifCinp ]CHIP IN $5 NOW

[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2151?c0=tbe-em-may23.oFPjTV&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230522_PM1_V1_S1&t=1006&akid=318%2E35263%2EifCinp ]CHIP IN $10 NOW

[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2152?c0=tbe-em-may23.TgCm-E&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230522_PM1_V1_S1&t=1007&akid=318%2E35263%2EifCinp ]CHIP IN $25 NOW

[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2153?c0=tbe-em-may23.6U9y2I&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230522_PM1_V1_S1&t=1008&akid=318%2E35263%2EifCinp ]CHIP IN $50 NOW

[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2154?c0=tbe-em-may23.N2RGls&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230522_PM1_V1_S1&t=1009&akid=318%2E35263%2EifCinp ]CHIP IN $100 NOW

[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2155?c0=tbe-em-may23.WiYufF&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230522_PM1_V1_S1&t=1010&akid=318%2E35263%2EifCinp ]ANOTHER AMOUNT

Thank you so much for pitching in today, if you can. I’ll keep you posted
on where we land!

— Lexie

Lexie Kelly
Finance Director
Tammy Baldwin for Senate

P.S. You’re a part of this team too, so we’d love your input on our
“question of the day.” If you could only have one type of coffee for the
rest of your life, would you pick hot coffee or iced coffee? You can reply
to this email with your answer to help us settle the debate!

P.O. BOX 696 MADISON, WI 53701

[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1002&akid=318%2E35263%2EifCinp ]www.tammybaldwin.com | [ mailto:INFO@tammybaldwin.com ]INFO@TAMMYBALDWIN.COM | [ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/68?t=1003&akid=318%2E35263%2EifCinp ]Privacy Policy

PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE

Peter, as the 2024 Presidential Election draws nearer, the stakes for our nation's future have never been higher.

Donald Trump spent four years nearly destroying our democracy, spreading hatred and division, and even inciting a deadly insurrection.

**And to top it off, he's now been indicted or is under investigation for multiple crimes in both federal and state courts.**

In short: Trump is too dangerous and unfit to serve as our commander-in-chief.

On the other hand, President Biden entered the oval office with a multitude of crises. The raging COVID pandemic, a broken economy, and our democracy in peril put our nation on edge by Trump’s extreme policies.

But that didn’t stop Joe from rolling up his sleeves to build America back.

His efforts have resulted in a stronger, more hopeful America today. And he’s delivering on his bold agenda. But all of this could be undone by a Trump re-election.

That’s why the potential 2024 rematch between Trump and President Biden won’t be just another election. It’s a historic fight for abortion rights, democracy, the freedom to vote, social security, healthcare benefits, and everything our country stands for.

It's time to ask ourselves: who do we want leading our country? Who will stand up for our values and protect the progress we've made?

**We must act now to show our support for President Biden and his bold agenda. That's why we're urgently polling voters in your area and counting on you to respond.**
Who will you vote for in 2024?
**[DONALD TRUMP](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63667740/407573890/-262845335?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc4OTg3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjhkNTFlMmNjLWRmZjgtZWQxMS05MDdjLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=YE4ucRxi2nzX764hV1KG83aDfLnJdXF_JDHrgj7mlj8=&emci=8c89a708-cff8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=8d51e2cc-dff8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=16951753)** | **[PRESIDENT BIDEN](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63667741/407573891/-262845335?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc4OTg3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjhkNTFlMmNjLWRmZjgtZWQxMS05MDdjLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=YE4ucRxi2nzX764hV1KG83aDfLnJdXF_JDHrgj7mlj8=&emci=8c89a708-cff8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=8d51e2cc-dff8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=16951753)**

clean_body: Don’t miss your chance to make your voice heard. | = URGENT: FLASH POLL FOR : Who will you vote for in 2024? | | ---|--- Peter, as the 2024 Presidential Election draws nearer, the stakes for our nation's future have never been higher. Donald Trump spent four years nearly destroying our democracy, spreading hatred and division, and even inciting a deadly insurrection. And to top it off, he's now been indicted or is under investigation for multiple crimes in both federal and state courts. In short: Trump is too dangerous and unfit to serve as our commander-in-chief. On the other hand, President Biden entered the oval office with a multitude of crises. The raging COVID pandemic, a broken economy, and our democracy in peril put our nation on edge by Trump’s extreme policies. But that didn’t stop Joe from rolling up his sleeves to build America back. His efforts have resulted in a stronger, more hopeful America today. And he’s delivering on his bold agenda. But all of this could be undone by a Trump re-election. That’s why the potential 2024 rematch between Trump and President Biden won’t be just another election. It’s a historic fight for abortion rights, democracy, the freedom to vote, social security, healthcare benefits, and everything our country stands for. It's time to ask ourselves: who do we want leading our country? Who will stand up for our values and protect the progress we've made? We must act now to show our support for President Biden and his bold agenda. That's why we're urgently polling voters in your area and counting on you to respond. Who will you vote for in 2024? | ---|--- We appreciate your input, Unite the Country | Unite the Country was formed in 2019 to support Joe Biden’s candidacy for President. After a resounding victory in 2020, we’ve been working around the clock to uplift President Biden’s message to the American electorate and elect Democrats who will continue to fight for his ambitious agenda. President Biden and the Democrats are fighting tooth-and-nail to move our country forward, but Republicans are threatening to derail everything. = | = = Follow along: | ---|--- | Paid for by Unite the Country, , and not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. name: Barbara Boxer via Blue Amp Action
subject: The rematch is on. Don’t sit this one out!
body:
Ken Calvert has to go
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[ unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5580acd6-f1bc-4791-b846-ca1c0843af63/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _._
---
|
---
| |
---
| | | | | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T473b4885-7670-4b80-bbdd-8b99306b18e4/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Peter, I’ve got a huge announcement that should bring a smile to your face and hope to your heart: former federal prosecutor, Will Rollins just announced his campaign for Congress in California’s 41st district -- one of the most important races in the country in the effort by Democrats to retake the House. Will stunned political experts in 2022 when he came so close to winning against right wing Republican, Ken Calvert. It ended up being one of the closest races in the country. He outperformed every projection and was the only Democratic challenger in California to win the independent vote. That’s a big deal. There is no question in my mind that in this upcoming election, with the Presidency on the ballot, the larger turnout will be the key to victory IF we have a strong campaign. I have committed my energy, time and money to this race and today I am asking you to start Will off with a show of support that will send him a message that we are behind him 100 percent. [I’ll share more about why I'm backing Will, but first, I have an important request: Could you split a $10 donation between Will’s campaign and Blue Amp Action to help him launch with strength? Will can win this thing, so please donate now.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T135440ec-dc9c-4617-80c0-dcbb06439b1b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
|
---
| | | | | | [**DONATE NOW**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5c5f2888-4ec9-47fd-8867-f1c347d8f552/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| Here’s why Democrats across the country should pay close attention to this race: 1\. We need candidates like Will in Congress. As a former federal prosecutor, he understands the ongoing danger that extremism poses to our democracy. He brought justice to insurrectionists, and I know he'll take the oath to defend democracy as seriously now as he did in the Justice Department. 2\. Retaking the House runs through CA-41. After the new lines were drawn, CA-41 became a true swing seat. It’s the type of district Democrats MUST WIN to regain Congress. 3\. GOP super PACs. Despite the tough midterm environment in 2022, Will was the third most “overperforming” Dem challenger in the country. He was also heavily outspent by the GOP’s dark money machine. If Democrats help Will keep pace, he can flip this seat! 4\. Will’s opponent Ken Calvert has got to go. Calvert voted to overturn the 2020 presidential election. He backs the extreme ban on abortion -- including for instances of rape, and wants doctors who perform them to go to jail. CA-41 is home to one of the most LGBTQ-friendly cities in Palm Springs, yet Calvert staunchly opposes gay marriage. The rematch is on, folks. Will came so close to winning in 2022, and this is our chance to finish the job. But it depends on widespread, grassroots support. [Please split a $10 contribution between Will Rollins’ campaign for Congress and Blue Amp Action today. I’m telling you, this will be one of the most critical races in the country, so don’t sit this one out! We need lots of Democrats to step up right out of the gate.](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tbfe44d22-0db1-45d5-9222-a3cc11c704dc/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Thanks, Barbara Boxer Former U.S. Senator, California

| | | | Cliff Schecter President, Blue Amp Action Amplify Good

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country.

name: Josh Harder
subject: protecting puppies
body:
It’s disgusting what some of these puppy mills are doing. | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63667913/407574415/904891240?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjRkZGIxNDgtZTBmOC1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Zy7Wz_G6yTuKB9M31A3s0XRnm56FrE3kEyF-qg949HQ=&emci=f1ddb148-e0f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=f4ddb148-e0f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=6455652) Peter, As you know, I love dogs. You might remember our campaign dog Cali - she’s as cute as ever and still very interested in treats. Dogs are a huge part of my life, Pam and I have fostered dozens of pups the past few years. We love it because there’s nothing that can give you more love and energy than a dog. **Recently I heard a story near my district that broke my heart though - over 150 dogs were rescued from a horrific puppy mill.** I genuinely couldn’t wrap my head around the fact that someone would intentionally hurt these dogs. So I started digging into it. Turns out, the Humane Society estimates there are currently 10,000 of these puppy mills across the United States! Many of these puppies are stuck in crates all day, never go outside, and are dying from malnutrition. It’s just terrible. Because of this research however, I’m proud to say you’re looking at the newest co-sponsor of the Puppy Protection Act. This is a super important bill that raises the legal standards to ensure all dogs are treated with basic necessities such as food, water, and vet care. [**If you agree this is something we need to pass, will you please add your name to support the Puppy Protection Act today?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63667914/407574416/904891240?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjRkZGIxNDgtZTBmOC1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Zy7Wz_G6yTuKB9M31A3s0XRnm56FrE3kEyF-qg949HQ=&emci=f1ddb148-e0f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=f4ddb148-e0f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=6455652) [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63667915/407574417/904891240?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjRkZGIxNDgtZTBmOC1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Zy7Wz_G6yTuKB9M31A3s0XRnm56FrE3kEyF-qg949HQ=&emci=f1ddb148-e0f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=f4ddb148-e0f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=6455652) And if you’re looking to have a 4-legged friend join your family, I also strongly encourage you to visit your local animal shelter. There are so many good dogs looking for their forever home. Send me your puppy pics if you do! \- Josh
---
* * *

Turns out, the Humane Society estimates there are currently 10,000 of these puppy mills across the United States! Many of these puppies are stuck in crates all day, never go outside, and are dying from malnutrition. It’s just terrible. Because of this research however, I’m proud to say you’re looking at the newest co-sponsor of the Puppy Protection Act. This is a super important bill that raises the legal standards to ensure all dogs are treated with basic necessities such as food, water, and vet care. And if you’re looking to have a 4-legged friend join your family, I also strongly encourage you to visit your local animal shelter. There are so many good dogs looking for their forever home. Send me your puppy pics if you do! \- Josh *

name: Fair Fight
subject: Republicans’ Board of Elections takeovers in Georgia: Explained
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FAIR FIGHT
body:
Brian Kemp just signed extremists’ Board of Elections takeover bills into law in three counties this month. |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63668803/407575997/1793776944?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20230522_f_boetakeovers_nor&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3YTIwOTIxZi1lMWY4LWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eXQTdcDUgS3--7mGhXFZC6iD-bNp8IHj6i3ybADBpbg=&emci=09bd3c50-d2f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=7a20921f-e1f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=1037277)

| | We want to talk about Governor Brian Kemp’s most recent anti-voter actions in Georgia and what Fair Fight is doing to mitigate their impact. **First, here’s the problem: Brian Kemp just signed extremists’ Board of Elections takeover bills into law in three counties this month — which would remove lawfully appointed Black members and Democrats from their ranks.** Among these laws is HB 422, which allowed the Republican-led Ware County Commission to remove Shawn Taylor, a Black Democrat, from the Board of Elections and replace her with a conservative board member. And this is part of a larger trend — in recent years, anti-voter Republicans in Georgia have introduced more than 18 bills aimed at dramatically altering the composition of local Boards of Elections. Following record-breaking turnout by Black, brown, and young voters since the 2020 presidential and 2021 runoff elections, these laws continue a string of coordinated efforts by Republicans nationwide to deny voters a voice and overturn the results of democratically held elections. As we gear up for the 2024 elections, where all eyes will be on Georgia once again, it’s clear that Republicans are laying the foundation NOW to maintain — and gain — their power through anti-democratic tactics. **Here’s how we’re fighting back: We’re reaching voters to educate them about their rights and mobilize them to push back against Republicans’ attempts to subvert elections.** It’s essential that voters in counties affected by Republican Board of Elections takeovers know how to get involved in the decisions that impact how they vote, when they vote, and how to ensure their votes count. Just this week, our efforts to advocate for pro-voter, diverse Board of Elections leadership paid off when Fulton County appointed Patrise Perkins-Hooker, a Black woman, as their Chair — after county commissioners attempted to rush the nomination process without any community input. Additionally, we’re continuing to train our Democracy Watch volunteers to advocate for voting access in County Board of Elections meetings, push back on anti-voter efforts, and monitor voter suppression efforts. **And here’s how you can help:** [_**In order to continue Fair Fight’s work to protect our democracy in Georgia, we rely on your support from emails just like this one. clean_body: Brian Kemp just signed extremists’ Board of Elections takeover bills into law in three counties this month. | | | We want to talk about Governor Brian Kemp’s most recent anti-voter actions in Georgia and what Fair Fight is doing to mitigate their impact. First, here’s the problem: Brian Kemp just signed extremists’ Board of Elections takeover bills into law in three counties this month — which would remove lawfully appointed Black members and Democrats from their ranks. Among these laws is HB 422, which allowed the Republican-led Ware County Commission to remove Shawn Taylor, a Black Democrat, from the Board of Elections and replace her with a conservative board member. And this is part of a larger trend — in recent years, anti-voter Republicans in Georgia have introduced more than 18 bills aimed at dramatically altering the composition of local Boards of Elections. Following record-breaking turnout by Black, brown, and young voters since the 2020 presidential and 2021 runoff elections, these laws continue a string of coordinated efforts by Republicans nationwide to deny voters a voice and overturn the results of democratically held elections. As we gear up for the 2024 elections, where all eyes will be on Georgia once again, it’s clear that Republicans are laying the foundation NOW to maintain — and gain — their power through anti-democratic tactics. Here’s how we’re fighting back: We’re reaching voters to educate them about their rights and mobilize them to push back against Republicans’ attempts to subvert elections. It’s essential that voters in counties affected by Republican Board of Elections takeovers know how to get involved in the decisions that impact how they vote, when they vote, and how to ensure their votes count. Just this week, our efforts to advocate for pro-voter, diverse Board of Elections leadership paid off when Fulton County appointed Patrise Perkins-Hooker, a Black woman, as their Chair — after county commissioners attempted to rush the nomination process without any community input. Additionally, we’re continuing to train our Democracy Watch volunteers to advocate for voting access in County Board of Elections meetings, push back on anti-voter efforts, and monitor voter suppression efforts. name: Team Engel
subject: Our opponent is threatening to tank the economy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Engel for Arizona
body:
https://www.engelforarizona.com/ [https://www.engelforarizona.com/]
Friend,
Right now, Congress and President Biden are locked in negotiation over the debt ceiling. There's a lot of finger-pointing going on, and we wanted to help clear that up.
The United States being in debt is nothing new. In fact, economists think it's generally a good thing.
Congress coming together to raise the debt ceiling is also nothing new. Since 1960, the debt ceiling has been raised by Congress 78 times — and 49 of those have been under Republican presidents!
But now, Republicans, including Kirsten's opponent, are holding the American people hostage. They're threatening to make us pay for the debt by slashing crucial programs — or they'll let the United States default on our debt, sending the global economy into a downward spiral.
They're playing politics — introducing absurd budget cuts that would only hurt their constituents in an effort to mess with Democrats.
At a time when Juan Ciscomani should be focused on investing in veterans' healthcare, strengthening our schools, and supporting our seniors, he's voting YES on a bill that would take away funding from all of those causes.
This vote was purely political. Ciscomani is exposing himself as someone who cares more about the agendas of extremist Republicans than the people who elected them.
Arizona's Sixth Congressional District deserves so much better than that. Kirsten Engel will actually fight for the people in Southern Arizona. Will you help us build as strong of a campaign as possible so we can elect Kirsten and get Ciscomani out of Congress? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel2024_foundingdonor?refcode=X_052223_DebtCeiling_SL1]

Thanks for all your support! Together, we'll flip this seat blue in November.
— Team Kirsten Engel for Arizona

She’s ready to take that fight to Washington to find common-sense solutions to the challenges we’re facing here in Arizona's Sixth Congressional District.
In 2022, Kirsten lost her race by one of the smallest margins in the country. Will you make sure she can build a winning campaign early by chipping in now? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel2024_foundingdonor?refcode=X_052223_DebtCeiling_SL1]

# Capitol Report
May 22, 2023

|
---
|
---
|

Jefferson City, MO 65101
(573) 751-4415
Steve.Roberts@senate.mo.gov

| | | | | | [](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=781ce7506b&e=a088917f89)

---
| | [](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=1369fc0bef&e=a088917f89)
---
|
---
| **Speaker says initiative limits intended to thwart abortion vote** In an extraordinary admission, House Speaker Dean Plocher on May 12 conceded Missouri voters “absolutely” will restore abortion rights if given the chance and that blocking them from doing so was majority Republicans’ motivation this year for trying to make it more difficult to use the initiative petition process to amend the state constitution.

Legislation imposing restrictions on the initiative was a top Republican priority but failed to win final passage after GOP infighting ground the Senate to a halt during the final days of the 2023 legislative session. Republicans increasingly have complained about the initiative after voters used it several times in recent years to bypass the legislature to enact policies – such as Medicaid expansion, legislative ethics reform and marijuana legalization – that enjoyed wide support among Missourians but were opposed by GOP lawmakers. During a news conference just hours before the legislative session ended, however, Plocher, R-Des Peres, admitted Republicans wanted to pass restrictions this year to thwart the restoration of abortion rights. Supporters of reproductive freedom are planning an initiative petition to put a proposed constitutional amendment on the 2024 statewide ballot to protect abortion rights at the state level after the U.S. Supreme Court’s far-right majority eliminated the national right to abortion last summer, allowing Missouri’s near-total ban on the procedure to take effect.

“I think we all believe that an initiative petition will be brought forth to allow choice,” Plocher said. “I believe it will pass. Absolutely.”

Since 1820, constitutional amendments have required just a simple majority of votes cast for ratification in Missouri. In its final form, House Joint Resolution 43 sought to raise the ratification threshold to 57 percent.

To trick voters into restricting their own power, the measure included a provision purporting to block non-citizens from voting on constitutional amendments. Since the Missouri Constitution already prohibits non-citizens from voting, the provision merely served as so-called “ballot candy” designed to deceive voters into supporting the measure even though they might oppose its main purpose. The legislatively drafted ballot language for HJR 43 listed the ballot candy first while mentioning the increased ratification threshold last.

The House of Representatives approved the final version of HJR 43 on May 9 by a near-party line vote of 107-55, with just four Republicans joining unanimous Democrats in opposition. The measure was one Senate vote away from going on the ballot but was never came up for debate after hardline conservatives spent most of the session’s last week holding the Senate floor to air various grievances, thus blocking the chamber from considering legislation. If HJR 43 had gone on the statewide ballot, ratification would have required a simple majority.
---
|
---
| **AG withdraws attempt to ban gender-affirming care**
Ahead of another expected defeat in court, Attorney General Andrew Bailey on May 16 quietly terminated an emergency administrative rule he sought to impose that essentially would have banned gender-affirming medical care in Missouri. A St. Louis County judge had already temporarily blocked the rule from taking effect and in doing so indicated Bailey’s action amounted to unconstitutional legislating by an executive branch official.

Circuit Judge Ellen Ribaudo blocked Bailey’s rule a day before it was slated to take effect April 27. Bailey, a Republican appointed to the office in January, claimed authority under the Missouri Merchandising Practices Act – a consumer protection law traditionally used to crack down on deceptive business practices or the sale of defective products – to regulate medical care for transgender patients. In her order blocking the rule, Ribaudo cast doubt on that claim, saying Bailey’s action “may impermissibly invade a function reserved for the legislature” by attempting to write substantive law under the guise of an administrative rule.

As of May 18, a hearing in the case was still scheduled for July 24. Since Bailey has withdrawn his rule, the judge could dismiss the case as moot. However, the plaintiffs, who include transgender patients and medical providers, might still push for a final ruling to prevent Bailey from pursuing similar actions in the future.

Hours after news of the rule’s withdrawal became public, Bailey issued a statement asserting the rule is no longer necessary now that the Republican-controlled legislature has passed legislation restricting gender-affirming health care for minors. However, while the legislation, Senate Bill 49, is limited to barring certain types of care only for those under age 18, Bailey’s rule was far more expansive and also would have applied to adults.

Gov. Mike Parson, a Republican, is expected to sign SB 49 into law. He is also expected to sign a companion measure, Senate Bill 39, passed this year to prohibit transgender children, as well as college athletes, from participating on sports teams that don’t correspond with their birth gender.
---
|
---
| **Governor signs $2 billion supplemental budget bill into law**
Gov. Mike Parson on May 15 signed into law a supplemental appropriations bill that authorizes more than $2 billion in additional state spending for the final months of the 2023 fiscal year, which ends June 30. Lawmakers granted final approval to the measure on May 5.

Major spending items in House Bill 15 include $425.16 million for the voter-approved expansion of Medicaid eligibility, $217.94 million for school nutrition programs, $119.14 million for child care services and $74.38 million in state funding for local charter schools.

The governor has yet to act on the various appropriations bills that make up the $51.51 billion state operating budget for the 2024 fiscal year. As a practical matter, Parson must sign those measures before the new fiscal year begins July 1.
---
|
---
| **House clears childcare property tax exemption for ballot**
The House of Representatives on May 12 voted 97-27, with 31 members voting “present,” to put a proposed constitutional amendment on the statewide ballot that would authorize the legislature to broadly exempt from taxation all real and personal property primarily used for child care. The Senate previously approved the measure in March on a 33-0 vote.

Senate Joint Resolution 26 would limit any tax exemptions to property used for child care outside of the home. As a result, families could not claim a tax exemption for property used to for in-home child care.

SJR 26 will automatically go on the November 2024 ballot for voter ratification, unless the governor exercises his constitutional prerogative to set an earlier election date.
---
|
---

[_**SB 64**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=03315dbefb&e=a088917f89) \- Enacts new law relating to wage range inquiries by employees and prospective employees

[**SB 65**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=56b809fc2a&e=a088917f89) \- Modifies provisions relating to the offense of unlawful use of weapons

[_**SB 145**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=fc28c708e5&e=a088917f89) \- Creates certificates of exemplary conduct and good moral character issued by circuit courts

[_**SB 146**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=0666db1b69&e=a088917f89) \- Creates provisions relating to compensation for wrongful convictions

[_**SB 147**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=2d8c40b9d5&e=a088917f89) \- Modifies provisions relating to parole eligibility

[_**SB 215**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=4178a924a4&e=a088917f89) \- Establishes provisions relating to alternative dispute resolution

[**_SB 216_**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=5ef5dfef8d&e=a088917f89) \- Designates "James W Brooks Memorial Highway" in St. Louis City and St. Louis County

**[_SB 217_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=b86d8a364a&e=a088917f89) **\- Creates the offense of unlawful possession by a minor

[_**SB 270**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=f72983806c&e=a088917f89) \- Modifies the practice of dentistry to include the prescription and administration of vaccines

[**SB 363**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=0302e044bc&e=a088917f89) \- Specifies that the St. Louis City school board shall fill any vacancy that occurs in such school board outside of the normal election cycle

[_**SJR 21**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=b04e0efcfa&e=a088917f89) \- Authorizes counties to freeze the real property assessed values of certain senior citizens

| **HELP FOR SMALL BUSINESS AND NON- PROFITS **
The U.S. Small Business Administration approved Missouri’s request for assistance through the federal agency’s disaster loan program. This move makes low-interest disaster loans available to small businesses and private non-profits severely impacted by the Coronavirus (COVID-19).

SBA’s Economic Injury Disaster Loans offer up to $2 million in assistance for each affected small business or non-profit. These loans can provide vital economic support to small businesses to help overcome the temporary loss of revenue they are experiencing.

Applicants will be able to apply online, receive additional disaster assistance information and [download applications](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=238fc16b10&e=a088917f89). Applicants may also call SBA’s Customer Service Center at (800) 659-2955 or email [disastercustomerservice@sba.gov](mailto:disastercustomerservice@sba.gov) for more information on SBA disaster assistance.
---
|
---
| **SAFE SHELTERING RESOURCES** Sheltering in place can be difficult for anyone, and some of our neighbors might not be safe due to violence at home. If you or someone you know needs help, please visit [Safe Connections](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=6ce7f8b5e4&e=a088917f89) or call _314-531-2003_.

You can also contact [ALIVE](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=d29864e41c&e=a088917f89) at _314-993-2777_.
---
|

| **RX OUTREACH EXPANDS ELIGIBILITY **
Below is a memo from a trusted non-profit:

As a non-profit, mail-order pharmacy, Rx Outreach is uniquely positioned to help stop the spread of COVID-19 for people who are quarantined and those who may be afraid to travel outside of their homes to the clinic or pharmacy.

Rx Outreach lifted its income guidelines during this crisis so that **anyone, regardless of income, can participate in their program.**

Rx Outreach medication prices are generally 70-80% below retail and are transparent. Shipping is free.

---
|
---
| **LOW-COST INSULIN** Before COVID-19 insulin users were having a difficult time affording medication due to the tripling of the cost over the last decade. In the wake of COVID-19, it has become even more difficult for those who rely on insulin to afford the inflated cost. Eli Lilly and Company has introduced a program to purchase Basaglar insulin at significant cost savings. Please call Lilly Diabetes Solution Center at 833-808-1234 between 9 am and 9 pm, M-F to see if you qualify for these savings. More information can be found at the [Eli Lilly and Co. Website](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=867d13c02a&e=a088917f89).

clean_body: | | | | | | | Capitol Report May 22, 2023 | | | | Contact Senator Roberts 201 W. Capitol Ave., Rm 329 Jefferson City, MO 65101 (573) 751-4415 Steve.Roberts@senate.mo.gov | | | | | | | | | | | | Speaker says initiative limits intended to thwart abortion vote In an extraordinary admission, House Speaker Dean Plocher on May 12 conceded Missouri voters “absolutely” will restore abortion rights if given the chance and that blocking them from doing so was majority Republicans’ motivation this year for trying to make it more difficult to use the initiative petition process to amend the state constitution. Legislation imposing restrictions on the initiative was a top Republican priority but failed to win final passage after GOP infighting ground the Senate to a halt during the final days of the 2023 legislative session. Republicans increasingly have complained about the initiative after voters used it several times in recent years to bypass the legislature to enact policies – such as Medicaid expansion, legislative ethics reform and marijuana legalization – that enjoyed wide support among Missourians but were opposed by GOP lawmakers. During a news conference just hours before the legislative session ended, however, Plocher, R-Des Peres, admitted Republicans wanted to pass restrictions this year to thwart the restoration of abortion rights. Supporters of reproductive freedom are planning an initiative petition to put a proposed constitutional amendment on the 2024 statewide ballot to protect abortion rights at the state level after the U.S. Supreme Court’s far-right majority eliminated the national right to abortion last summer, allowing Missouri’s near-total ban on the procedure to take effect. “I think we all believe that an initiative petition will be brought forth to allow choice,” Plocher said. “I believe it will pass. Absolutely.” Since 1820, constitutional amendments have required just a simple majority of votes cast for ratification in Missouri. In its final form, House Joint Resolution 43 sought to raise the ratification threshold to 57 percent. To trick voters into restricting their own power, the measure included a provision purporting to block non-citizens from voting on constitutional amendments. Since the Missouri Constitution already prohibits non-citizens from voting, the provision merely served as so-called “ballot candy” designed to deceive voters into supporting the measure even though they might oppose its main purpose. The legislatively drafted ballot language for HJR 43 listed the ballot candy first while mentioning the increased ratification threshold last. The House of Representatives approved the final version of HJR 43 on May 9 by a near-party line vote of 107-55, with just four Republicans joining unanimous Democrats in opposition. The measure was one Senate vote away from going on the ballot but was never came up for debate after hardline conservatives spent most of the session’s last week holding the Senate floor to air various grievances, thus blocking the chamber from considering legislation. If HJR 43 had gone on the statewide ballot, ratification would have required a simple majority. | | AG withdraws attempt to ban gender-affirming care Ahead of another expected defeat in court, Attorney General Andrew Bailey on May 16 quietly terminated an emergency administrative rule he sought to impose that essentially would have banned gender-affirming medical care in Missouri. A St. Louis County judge had already temporarily blocked the rule from taking effect and in doing so indicated Bailey’s action amounted to unconstitutional legislating by an executive branch official. Circuit Judge Ellen Ribaudo blocked Bailey’s rule a day before it was slated to take effect April 27. Bailey, a Republican appointed to the office in January, claimed authority under the Missouri Merchandising Practices Act – a consumer protection law traditionally used to crack down on deceptive business practices or the sale of defective products – to regulate medical care for transgender patients. In her order blocking the rule, Ribaudo cast doubt on that claim, saying Bailey’s action “may impermissibly invade a function reserved for the legislature” by attempting to write substantive law under the guise of an administrative rule. As of May 18, a hearing in the case was still scheduled for July 24. Since Bailey has withdrawn his rule, the judge could dismiss the case as moot. However, the plaintiffs, who include transgender patients and medical providers, might still push for a final ruling to prevent Bailey from pursuing similar actions in the future. Hours after news of the rule’s withdrawal became public, Bailey issued a statement asserting the rule is no longer necessary now that the Republican-controlled legislature has passed legislation restricting gender-affirming health care for minors. However, while the legislation, Senate Bill 49, is limited to barring certain types of care only for those under age 18, Bailey’s rule was far more expansive and also would have applied to adults. Gov. Mike Parson, a Republican, is expected to sign SB 49 into law. He is also expected to sign a companion measure, Senate Bill 39, passed this year to prohibit transgender children, as well as college athletes, from participating on sports teams that don’t correspond with their birth gender. | | Governor signs $2 billion supplemental budget bill into law Gov. Mike Parson on May 15 signed into law a supplemental appropriations bill that authorizes more than $2 billion in additional state spending for the final months of the 2023 fiscal year, which ends June 30. Lawmakers granted final approval to the measure on May 5. Major spending items in House Bill 15 include $425.16 million for the voter-approved expansion of Medicaid eligibility, $217.94 million for school nutrition programs, $119.14 million for child care services and $74.38 million in state funding for local charter schools. The governor has yet to act on the various appropriations bills that make up the $51.51 billion state operating budget for the 2024 fiscal year. As a practical matter, Parson must sign those measures before the new fiscal year begins July 1. | | House clears childcare property tax exemption for ballot The House of Representatives on May 12 voted 97-27, with 31 members voting “present,” to put a proposed constitutional amendment on the statewide ballot that would authorize the legislature to broadly exempt from taxation all real and personal property primarily used for child care. The Senate previously approved the measure in March on a 33-0 vote. Senate Joint Resolution 26 would limit any tax exemptions to property used for child care outside of the home. As a result, families could not claim a tax exemption for property used to for in-home child care. SJR 26 will automatically go on the November 2024 ballot for voter ratification, unless the governor exercises his constitutional prerogative to set an earlier election date. | | 2023 SENATE BILLS \- Creates new provisions relating to financial institutions \- Enacts new law relating to wage range inquiries by employees and prospective employees \- Modifies provisions relating to the offense of unlawful use of weapons \- Creates certificates of exemplary conduct and good moral character issued by circuit courts \- Creates provisions relating to compensation for wrongful convictions \- Modifies provisions relating to parole eligibility \- Establishes provisions relating to alternative dispute resolution \- Designates "James W Brooks Memorial Highway" in St. Louis City and St. Louis County \- Creates the offense of unlawful possession by a minor \- Modifies the practice of dentistry to include the prescription and administration of vaccines \- Specifies that the St. Louis City school board shall fill any vacancy that occurs in such school board outside of the normal election cycle \- Authorizes counties to freeze the real property assessed values of certain senior citizens | | "Signed By Governor" | "Truly Agreed Bills" | | | | | HELP FOR SMALL BUSINESS AND NON- PROFITS The U.S. Small Business Administration approved Missouri’s request for assistance through the federal agency’s disaster loan program. This move makes low-interest disaster loans available to small businesses and private non-profits severely impacted by the Coronavirus (COVID-19). SBA’s Economic Injury Disaster Loans offer up to $2 million in assistance for each affected small business or non-profit. These loans can provide vital economic support to small businesses to help overcome the temporary loss of revenue they are experiencing. Applicants will be able to apply online, receive additional disaster assistance information and . Applicants may also call SBA’s Customer Service Center at (800) 659-2955 or email mailto:disastercustomerservice@sba.gov for more information on SBA disaster assistance. | | SAFE SHELTERING RESOURCES Sheltering in place can be difficult for anyone, and some of our neighbors might not be safe due to violence at home. If you or someone you know needs help, please visit or call 314-531-2003. You can also contact at 314-993-2777. | | COMMUNITY RESOURCE LIST The has a COVID-19 Community Economic Relief Fund. Rx Outreach medication prices are generally 70-80% below retail and are transparent. Shipping is free. | | LOW-COST INSULIN Before COVID-19 insulin users were having a difficult time affording medication due to the tripling of the cost over the last decade. voter turnout is on the rise

| | Peter, in spite of Brian Kemp and Brad Raffensperger’s efforts, Georgia saw increased turnout in the 2022 midterm elections.

Thanks to the support of our grassroots team, we were able to make sure that strong Democrats knew when, where, and how to vote – and we made sure that every Democratic voter knew just how important it was that we re-elect Rev. Warnock.

Our efforts were successful, and we re-elected Rev. Warnock, maintaining our Senate majority.

**_We know our work matters, and we know that it’s effective at boosting voter turnout._**

Right now, we’re prepping for the 2024 presidential election because we know that turnout is everything when it comes to winning in Georgia.

It’s simple: if Georgia Democrats turn out in full force again to vote for Joe Biden, then we’ll hold the White House.

But if Republicans turn out more of their voters, we’ll lose, and Trump will return to the White House, ready to wreak even more havoc on the country.

[**_We know our strategy for turning out voters is effective, and we’ve proven it time and again with our historic wins in 2020, 2021, and 2022. Will you pitch in to support our hard work ahead of 2024?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63669036/407576843/702231530?refcode=20230522.2_GTP_5AS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmM2ZkMjIzOC1lM2Y4LWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Q6AHw00SnznXCO2fr5rc8lDepoQQOECyJNeOQpNN4cY=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: voter turnout is on the rise | | | Peter, in spite of Brian Kemp and Brad Raffensperger’s efforts, Georgia saw increased turnout in the 2022 midterm elections. Thanks to the support of our grassroots team, we were able to make sure that strong Democrats knew when, where, and how to vote – and we made sure that every Democratic voter knew just how important it was that we re-elect Rev. Warnock. Our efforts were successful, and we re-elected Rev. Warnock, maintaining our Senate majority. We know our work matters, and we know that it’s effective at boosting voter turnout. Right now, we’re prepping for the 2024 presidential election because we know that turnout is everything when it comes to winning in Georgia. It’s simple: if Georgia Democrats turn out in full force again to vote for Joe Biden, then we’ll hold the White House. But if Republicans turn out more of their voters, we’ll lose, and Trump will return to the White House, ready to wreak even more havoc on the country. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = = = = With your support, we’ll continue to run the top voter outreach and voter protection program in the country, and we WILL win in 2024. | | It's been over 100 years since Congress ratified the 19th Amendment, ensuring women the constitutional right to vote. But decades later, women still don't have equal protection under the Constitution. **Many states still have laws in place that discriminate against women, and the worst part is – those laws are still constitutional.** And just days ago, Republicans on the Senate floor blocked the Equal Rights Amendment, which would have guaranteed women equal protection under the Constitution. **It's ludicrous that more than 100 years after guaranteeing women the right to vote, we are still debating whether or not to protect women from discrimination under the Constitution.** This shouldn't be a partisan issue. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63669064/407576929/-2044921950?refcode=2023-05-22-era&recurring=&amount=7&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=cfdf024c-e3f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZmRmMDI0Yy1lM2Y4LWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Eu4cEDB75rGHZC5Ke79Hk26r7sGAmx-laytVPkJB3u4=) Between blocking the Equal Rights Amendment, ripping away reproductive freedom, voting against equal pay, and more, **Republicans in Congress have made it clear they'll stop at nothing when it comes to blocking progress on women's rights.** It should go without saying that our nation's elected officials will vote to end discrimination based on gender, race, age, and sexual orientation. But just like so many other things, Republicans have weaponized this issue with their partisan talking points instead of doing what's right for our country. **[We need to expand our Senate majority so we can continue to build on progress, not turn back the clock to yesteryear. Help defend and expand our Senate majority from Republican attacks with an $7 donation.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63669065/407576930/-2044921950?refcode=2023-05-22-era&recurring=&amount=7&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=cfdf024c-e3f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZmRmMDI0Yy1lM2Y4LWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Eu4cEDB75rGHZC5Ke79Hk26r7sGAmx-laytVPkJB3u4=)** [Donate Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63669066/407576931/-2044921950?refcode=2023-05-22-era&recurring=&amount=7&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=cfdf024c-e3f8-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZmRmMDI0Yy1lM2Y4LWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Eu4cEDB75rGHZC5Ke79Hk26r7sGAmx-laytVPkJB3u4=) Together we can create a world where we not only protect women's rights but work to expand them. [**May 22, 1849:** Abraham Lincoln, at age 40, received U.S. patent #6469 for his invention of a device to lift boats over shoals. During a trip home from Congress, he was inspired when his boat was stuck on a sandbar, but the future president never went on to construct the design. He is the only U.S. president to have held a patent in his name.](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=0bcd80d2ff9b4b53a2a2cb165d1e3f87&_e=0PtN9scDKKwOln_WIFtRuRDiTY4qYjo0rTJ_u1nnMTMTZ3XbYzlcaIfRsPjrDvpUKYcn5Zh-TWRhpj2rvE4Xokh1NsGPI3C0Y5Y5F-hS1ti6kU9guUHKJXy4s7DOAH7fQdj64ejxYE0MnCJNvg6K-96NjIdaqnbMCjz8h9P7EFKFlhMCUVazjNSor0L3hFWe5LQKNYSVrpRFLc_YKwBDxHNcbisadwpcM-zPup8tyW6evIDidM34WhOs4pl8Cvzj) **Source -[abrahamlincolnonline.org](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=0bcd80d2ff9b4b53a2a2cb165d1e3f87&_e=0PtN9scDKKwOln_WIFtRuRDiTY4qYjo0rTJ_u1nnMTMTZ3XbYzlcaIfRsPjrDvpUKYcn5Zh-TWRhpj2rvE4Xokh1NsGPI3C0Y5Y5F-hS1tgr-oW8FlVAMccD7Zl0lmpVPpnJd1sl_KSOcB_leEKScggHqFR3LwomdEV4OjD9LVnVpoltEgaSSTY98jrRJyhQYHdRqC2ZGBpLG5F9mv5nah_J-KaW0PdGeVHorO5bs8lqCNtBN4Eau7_oTDEI-oIR)**

[**May 22, 1972:** President Richard Nixon became the first sitting U.S. president to visit the Soviet Union with his arrival in Moscow. He spent a week attending summit meetings with Soviet leader Leonid Brezhnev and others, where they reached several significant agreements, including laying the groundwork for the 1975 joint space flight. Nixon had previously visited as Vice President under President Eisenhower.](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=0bcd80d2ff9b4b53a2a2cb165d1e3f87&_e=xUWXa0K0ZyFPi4GbzREI8JkKOQxuqiQrHj4EGcAwdvFXCM8kpW69ixjIcombir6xk8AWQNYCE9idOeiHXi7R-LmMJXxw6sgeTFUYc1Z3wn6c3Ta0U-zBtFrcahoIRPHg4QW4HGf8mYpgB48lqabsch7trp4J9WghfPFgCr9MkRodI0Qtv_5Y72vN0XTucrDVaKEioJySqiYGxm1X31SXPAa81-v86OyaG83kKFsAjYbjMNDB1bt5ZCJPMVXuJ9B0RfFWfHwPdj3vSeMxD0akLQ%3D%3D) **Source -[history.com](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=0bcd80d2ff9b4b53a2a2cb165d1e3f87&_e=xUWXa0K0ZyFPi4GbzREI8JkKOQxuqiQrHj4EGcAwdvFXCM8kpW69ixjIcombir6xk8AWQNYCE9idOeiHXi7R-LmMJXxw6sgeTFUYc1Z3wn6p7O1ni2_D-QSWM9y1yO6mJzHSByTGNDTw_RBliw819bv50qbliTAH8vA-klVZLKjomUVmtaILS5of_JU_5hHJJlO8x2X5-NZEASuRLiOkXEFTZNjGU8u2Gb9Piw0PpExUUPiaTrR2-DD_s9GM1Eqi6MR15W_HA9hmrkMjVKjGvA%3D%3D)**
